rulings.Case Title: TATIANA SANOCHKINA et al VS. MATHEW HUI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 22, 2014, Line 17: DEFENDANTS DAN MCLEAN, JESUS MAREZ, and ZEPHYR REAL ESTATE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend based upon the doctrine of res judicata. See Thibodeau v. Crum (1992) 4 Cal.App.4th 749, 754-755; CCP 430.10(e). The moving defendants are privies of Mr. Yuen and the arbitrator already ruled that plaintiff did not sustain any damages when she purchased the property. rulings.Case Title: HUBERT WONG et al VS. CALLAHAN, MCCUNE & WILLIS, PLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order Re Pltfs' Written Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, July 22, 2014, Line 1,DEFENDANTs CALLAHAN, MCCUNE & WILLIS, PLC, PETER CALLAHAN, LEEH DIBELLO, ANNA GEHRIGER's Motion For Protective Order Re Plaintiff's Written Discovery Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion for protective order is granted. Defendants to serve verified responses and produce documents no later than August 29, 2014. The court reminds defendants that they have a good faith obligation to obtain information from sources under their control. See Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 C3d 771, 782. Defendants are also obligated to do a diligent search and make a reasonable inquiry in responding to the document requests. See CCP 2031.230. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PRINCE OF PEACE ENTERPRISES, INC. VS. WHITE SNOW CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 22, 2014, Line 15: CROSS DEFENDANT PRINCE OF PEACE ENTERPRISES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Cross-Complainant failed in three attempts to sufficiently plead negligent representation with the requisite specificity. (CACI no. 1903. See SAXC, 18-30.) Further, Cross-Complainant failed to allege that Cross-Defendant lacked a reasonable ground to believe its statements. (See Century Surety Co. v. Crosby Ins. Inc. (2004) 124 Cal.App.4th 116, 129.) Additionally, the Second Amended Cross Complaint alleged that White Snow-rather than Mr. Eng-was the purchaser of the consumer products and has therefore failed to allege the required elements of a cause of action for breach of the implied warranty of merchantability with respect to him personally. (CACI no. 3210.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY HEFFERNAN VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, AN AGENCY OF THE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate/Alt Petition For Writ Of Prohibition From The Suspension Of Petitioner'S California Drivers License And Certified Administrative Record
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 22, 2014, Line 2: PETITIONER TIMOTHY HEFFERNAN'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate/Alternative Petition For Writ Of Prohibition From The Suspension Of Petitioner's California Drivers License And Certified Administrative Record. Denied. Petitioner has not established by the preponderance of the evidence that the administrative findings are contrary to the weight of the evidence. The evidence supports the hearing officer's conclusion, as it establishes that Officer Pettit read Petitioner the chemical test admonishment from the DS-367 and Officer Pettit repeatedly attempted to advise petitioner that the right to counsel did not apply to his decision regarding whether to submit to chemical testing. rulings.Case Title: DENNIS RUSH VS. WHITE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 22, 2014, Line 5: DEFENDANT TREADWELL & ROLLO, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment and adjudication is denied. Defendant owed plaintiff a duty of care as a matter of law. Plaintiff's evidence demonstrates that defendant had a key role in the development of the project which brings this case under the Beacon Res. Comm. Ass'n v. Skidmore, Owings & Merrill LLP line of authority. Defendant drafted a memorandum regarding the disclosures. (O'Conner dec., Ex.17). Defendant issued a memo stating: "The purpose of our services is to (1) work with the design team and contractor¿and (2) observe the construction/installation of the geotechnical related elements of the project to check they are constructed in accordance with the intent of our recommendation." (O'Conner dec., Ex.8). One of defendant's emails states that defendant is "currently providing ongoing recommendations for the 13 cabins, as they are being constructed." (O'Conner dec., Ex.5). Defendant states that it "intend[s] to address several issues key to developing the site." (O'Conner dec., Ex.2). Defendant was performing ongoing consultation during the construction. (O'Conner dec., Ex.11). Defendant and the developer (Dolan) worked together to determine what disclosures were made. (O'Conner dec., Ex. 9). In one e-mail, the developer wrote defendant and said don't tell us the bad news, I'm not sure we can handle the truth. (O'Conner dec., Ex.16). The developer also paid defendant several hundreds of thousands of dollar for defendant's services. (O'Conner dec., Exs. 21-26). In sum, defendant's extensive role with the development and substantial payment distinguishes this case from Weseloh Family Ltd. Partnership v. K.L. Wessel Constr. Co. , Inc. (2004) 125 Cal.App.4th 152, and Beacon is controlling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES H LIU MD VS. YORK H LIU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 22, 2014, Line 7: CROSS DEFENDANT JAMES LIU'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled. A cross-complaint that is subject-matter related to the plaintiff's complaint relates back to when the action was commenced for statute of limitations purposes. (Sidney v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 710, 714.) The First Amended Cross Complaint ("FACC") alleges that the injury occurred on December 24, 2010. The underlying complaint was filed on December 24, 2012. The three year statute of limitations provided by CCP sec. 340.5 had not yet expired at the time of the filing of the underlying complaint and the FACC is therefore timely. rulings.Case Title: CARMEN DIAZ ECHAURI VS. EKO VENTURE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Subpoenas And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, July 22, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF CARMEN ECHAURI's Motion To Quash Subpoenas And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to quash is denied. Personnel records come within the zone of privacy protected by Article I, § 1 of the California Constitution. Board of Trustees v. Superior Court (1981) 119 CA3d 516 However, the privilege is not absolute. When an individual's right of privacy in his personal affairs conflicts with the public need for discovery in litigation, the competing interests must be carefully balanced. See Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 C.3d 391 The court finds that the issues regarding plaintiff's past job performances are relevant to the subject matter of the litigation and defendant's right to discovery outweighs plaintiff's privacy rights. See Bridgestone-Firestone Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 7 CA4th 1384, 1392. The subpoenaed documents are to be produced under a protective order for "attorney's eyes only." The court further finds that plaintiff failed to meet and confer in good faith. Defendants' request for sanctions against plaintiff Carmen Diaz Echauri is granted. Sanctions in the amount of $2,100.00 are to be made payable to Eko Venture, Inc. and their attorneys Gordon and Rees and delivered no later than August 5, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CITI-NETWORKING GROUP, INC VS. DERRICK A RAMIREZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction (Ex Parte Papers Are Deemed Moving Papers; Opposition Due 7/07; Reply Due 7/09)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, July 22, 2014, Line 16: Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction. Granted in part and denied in part. The temporary restraining order requiring Defendant Derrick A. Ramirez to preserve evidence shall remain in effect during the pendency of these proceedings. All other requested relief is denied. Plaintiff has not established that it has an ownership interest in the equipment purchased and configured by Defendant and therefore, has not established a likelihood of success on the merits. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)
